trans-unit.source: Digitisation
trans-unit.target: Digitisation

trans-unit.source: Digitisation is the process of converting geographic data from maps or images into a digital form commonly represented as vector data. Proficiency in digitisation stands as a cornerstone for GIS specialists. It empowers them to convert spatial information into a digital format, facilitating more efficient data manipulation compared to traditional methods of interpreting images or paper maps.
trans-unit.target: Digitisation is the process of converting geographic data from maps or images into a digital form commonly represented as vector data. Proficiency in digitisation stands as a cornerstone for GIS specialists. It empowers them to convert spatial information into a digital format, facilitating more efficient data manipulation compared to traditional methods of interpreting images or paper maps.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Digitisation in humanitarian work :class: tip
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Digitisation in humanitarian work :class: tip

trans-unit.source: If you want to know how community mapping and digitisation can help improve the resilience of communities and facilitate humanitarian work, take a look at Paul Knight's blogpost [“This is the first time this community is on a map…” — Digital Community Mapping in Nigeria](https://medium.com/digital-and-innovation-at-british-red-cross/first-time-this-community-has-been-on-a-map-nigeria-f592906b7be1).
trans-unit.target: If you want to know how community mapping and digitisation can help improve the resilience of communities and facilitate humanitarian work, take a look at Paul Knight's blogpost [“This is the first time this community is on a map…” — Digital Community Mapping in Nigeria](https://medium.com/digital-and-innovation-at-british-red-cross/first-time-this-community-has-been-on-a-map-nigeria-f592906b7be1).

trans-unit.source: The concept of digitisation within GIS (Source: HeiGIT). :::
trans-unit.target: The concept of digitisation within GIS (Source: HeiGIT). :::

trans-unit.source: Digitising in QGIS
trans-unit.target: Digitising in QGIS

trans-unit.source: In QGIS,digitisation involves tracing features such as points, lines, or polygons directly on the map canvas to represent geographic elements like buildings, roads, or land parcels. Additionally, you can assign attributes to each feature during digitization, enabling further analysis and integration with other geospatial data. This digitized data becomes a valuable asset for spatial analysis and mapping.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS,digitisation involves tracing features such as points, lines, or polygons directly on the map canvas to represent geographic elements like buildings, roads, or land parcels. Additionally, you can assign attributes to each feature during digitization, enabling further analysis and integration with other geospatial data. This digitized data becomes a valuable asset for spatial analysis and mapping.

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify sd-text-black sd-rounded-3 sd-border-2 __Real world Scenario 1/3__ ^^^ There has been a flood in a village following heavy rains. To assess the needs of the household and the impact on the infrastructure, you are tasked to make an overview map of the region and mark the flood impacted buildings and roads. However, there are no datasets with the buildings or roads available. For the map, you will have to create two new layers with the road network and the buildings. However, there is recent satellite imagery available. With these images, you can create vector layers representing the key infrastructure, such as buildings and roads. Once you have created the layers, you can create a preliminary overview map of the village. This map will be given out to community members and volunteers on the ground to map out the damaged infrastructure. In a next step, the information collected by the ground team can be used to enrich the data and create a flood impact assessment. To create the map, you will need to create new vector layers. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify sd-text-black sd-rounded-3 sd-border-2 __Real world Scenario 1/3__ ^^^ There has been a flood in a village following heavy rains. To assess the needs of the household and the impact on the infrastructure, you are tasked to make an overview map of the region and mark the flood impacted buildings and roads. However, there are no datasets with the buildings or roads available. For the map, you will have to create two new layers with the road network and the buildings. However, there is recent satellite imagery available. With these images, you can create vector layers representing the key infrastructure, such as buildings and roads. Once you have created the layers, you can create a preliminary overview map of the village. This map will be given out to community members and volunteers on the ground to map out the damaged infrastructure. In a next step, the information collected by the ground team can be used to enrich the data and create a flood impact assessment. To create the map, you will need to create new vector layers. :::

trans-unit.source: Digitisation Toolbars
trans-unit.target: Digitisation Toolbars

trans-unit.source: The Digitisation Toolbar in QGIS 3.36. :::
trans-unit.target: The Digitisation Toolbar in QGIS 3.36. :::

trans-unit.source: Digitising is done with the `Digitizing Toolbar` and on the map canvas. First, you need to check if the `Digitizing Toolbar` is activated. To do that:
trans-unit.target: Digitising is done with the `Digitizing Toolbar` and on the map canvas. First, you need to check if the `Digitizing Toolbar` is activated. To do that:

trans-unit.source: Click on the `View` tab in the menu bar and click `Toolbars`. Check if the `Digitizing` and `Advanced Digitizing` toolbar is activated.
trans-unit.target: Click on the `View` tab in the menu bar and click `Toolbars`. Check if the `Digitizing` and `Advanced Digitizing` toolbar is activated.

trans-unit.source: First, you need to check if the `Digitizing Toolbox` is activated. To do that
trans-unit.target: First, you need to check if the `Digitizing Toolbox` is activated. To do that

trans-unit.source: Click on the `View` tab in the menu bar and click `Toolbars`. Check if the `Digitizing` and `Advanced Digitizing` toolbox is activated.
trans-unit.target: Click on the `View` tab in the menu bar and click `Toolbars`. Check if the `Digitizing` and `Advanced Digitizing` toolbox is activated.

trans-unit.source: A tool box like this should appear on top of the QGIS interface
trans-unit.target: A tool box like this should appear on top of the QGIS interface

trans-unit.source: The digitisation toolbar offers the basic tools to create, save, and edit features. However, for everything that goes above just creating new features and deleting features, the Advanced Digitization toolbar is needed (see {numref}`digitising_toolbar`). The Advanced Digitization toolbar allows you to move features, delete parts of features, and much more. All functions are listed in the two tables below.
trans-unit.target: The digitisation toolbar offers the basic tools to create, save, and edit features. However, for everything that goes above just creating new features and deleting features, the Advanced Digitization toolbar is needed (see {numref}`digitising_toolbar`). The Advanced Digitization toolbar allows you to move features, delete parts of features, and much more. All functions are listed in the two tables below.

trans-unit.source: Digitizing Toolbar in QGIS 3.36. :::
trans-unit.target: Digitizing Toolbar in QGIS 3.36. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Digitisation Toolbar | Tool | Purpose | Tool | Purpose | |---|---|-----|---| | ![](/fig/mActionAllEdits.png) | Access to save, rollback or cancel changes in all or selected layers simultaneously | ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) | Turn edit status of selected layer(s) on or off. | | ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) |Save edits | | | ![](/fig/mActionDigitizeWithSegment.png) | Digitise using straight segments | ![](/fig/mActionDigitizeWithCurve.png) | Digitize using curve lines | | ![](/fig/mActionStreamingDigitize.png) | Enable freehand digitising|![](/fig/mActionDigitizeShape.png) |Digitise polygon of regular shape | | ![](/fig/mActionNewTableRow.png) | Add new record | ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png) | Add Feature: Capture Point | | ![](/fig/mActionCaptureLine.png) | Add Feature: Capture Line | ![](/fig/mActionCapturePolygon.png) | Add Feature: Capture Polygon | | ![](/fig/mActionVertexTool.png) | Vertex Tool (All Layers) | ![](/fig/mActionVertexToolActiveLayer.png) |Vertex Tool (Current Layer) | | ![](/fig/checkbox.png) | Set whether the vertex editor panel should auto-open | ![](/fig/mActionMultiEdit.png) | Modify the attributes of all selected features simultaneously | | ![](/fig/mActionDeleteSelectedFeatures.png) | Delete selected features from the active layer | ![](/fig/mActionEditCut.png) | Cut features from the active layer | | ![](/fig/mActionCopySelected.png) | Copy selected features from the active layer | ![](/fig/mActionEditPaste.png) | Paste features into the active layer | | ![](/fig/mActionUndo.png) | Undo changes in the active layer | ![](/fig/mActionRedo.png) | Redo changes in active layer |
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Digitisation Toolbar | Tool | Purpose | Tool | Purpose | |---|---|-----|---| | ![](/fig/mActionAllEdits.png) | Access to save, rollback or cancel changes in all or selected layers simultaneously | ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) | Turn edit status of selected layer(s) on or off. | | ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) |Save edits | | | ![](/fig/mActionDigitizeWithSegment.png) | Digitise using straight segments | ![](/fig/mActionDigitizeWithCurve.png) | Digitize using curve lines | | ![](/fig/mActionStreamingDigitize.png) | Enable freehand digitising|![](/fig/mActionDigitizeShape.png) |Digitise polygon of regular shape | | ![](/fig/mActionNewTableRow.png) | Add new record | ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png) | Add Feature: Capture Point | | ![](/fig/mActionCaptureLine.png) | Add Feature: Capture Line | ![](/fig/mActionCapturePolygon.png) | Add Feature: Capture Polygon | | ![](/fig/mActionVertexTool.png) | Vertex Tool (All Layers) | ![](/fig/mActionVertexToolActiveLayer.png) |Vertex Tool (Current Layer) | | ![](/fig/checkbox.png) | Set whether the vertex editor panel should auto-open | ![](/fig/mActionMultiEdit.png) | Modify the attributes of all selected features simultaneously | | ![](/fig/mActionDeleteSelectedFeatures.png) | Delete selected features from the active layer | ![](/fig/mActionEditCut.png) | Cut features from the active layer | | ![](/fig/mActionCopySelected.png) | Copy selected features from the active layer | ![](/fig/mActionEditPaste.png) | Paste features into the active layer | | ![](/fig/mActionUndo.png) | Undo changes in the active layer | ![](/fig/mActionRedo.png) | Redo changes in active layer |

trans-unit.source: For more complex digitisation procedures, you will use the advanced digitisation toolbar. However, for this chapter, we will focus on the normal digitisation toolbar.
trans-unit.target: For more complex digitisation procedures, you will use the advanced digitisation toolbar. However, for this chapter, we will focus on the normal digitisation toolbar.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Advanced digitizing Toolbar
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Advanced digitizing Toolbar

trans-unit.source: Tool
trans-unit.target: Tool

trans-unit.source: Purpose
trans-unit.target: Purpose

trans-unit.source: Tool
trans-unit.target: Tool

trans-unit.source: Purpose
trans-unit.target: Purpose

trans-unit.target: Enable Advanced Digitizing Tools

trans-unit.target: Move Feature(s)

trans-unit.source: Trim or Extend Feature
trans-unit.target: Trim or Extend Feature

trans-unit.source: Creating new datasets
trans-unit.target: Creating new datasets

trans-unit.source: To digitise new data, you always have to start with creating the dataset before filling it with digitised data. Keep in mind that one layer can only contain one type of geometry: either points, lines, or polygons. When you create a dataset, you will have to choose the type of geometry. Additionally, you can add further columns with attributes to add more data to the data table.
trans-unit.target: To digitise new data, you always have to start with creating the dataset before filling it with digitised data. Keep in mind that one layer can only contain one type of geometry: either points, lines, or polygons. When you create a dataset, you will have to choose the type of geometry. Additionally, you can add further columns with attributes to add more data to the data table.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn! :class: note
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn! :class: note

trans-unit.source: Think of a spatial dataset you could need in your humanitarian operations. What kind of additional information is useful? How would you collect it? This could range from type of road, crops planted, type of vegetation or social indicators. You can discuss in groups and write it down on paper or add it to a digital whiteboard.
trans-unit.target: Think of a spatial dataset you could need in your humanitarian operations. What kind of additional information is useful? How would you collect it? This could range from type of road, crops planted, type of vegetation or social indicators. You can discuss in groups and write it down on paper or add it to a digital whiteboard.

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Video: How to create a new dataset
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Video: How to create a new dataset

trans-unit.source: `Layer` → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer` or `New Shapefile Layer`.
trans-unit.target: `Layer` → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer` or `New Shapefile Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to the `file name` input field and navigate to the folder where you want to save the dataset.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to the `file name` input field and navigate to the folder where you want to save the dataset.

trans-unit.source: `File encoding`: Make sure this is set to UTF-8.
trans-unit.target: `File encoding`: Make sure this is set to UTF-8.

trans-unit.source: `Geometry type`: Select the type of feature you want to digitise e.g. points or lines.
trans-unit.target: `Geometry type`: Select the type of feature you want to digitise e.g. points or lines.

trans-unit.source: Under `Additional dimension` you should always make sure that you check `None`. Except if there is the possibility to collect the Z-values (elevation) as well. But this is mostly not the case.
trans-unit.target: Under `Additional dimension` you should always make sure that you check `None`. Except if there is the possibility to collect the Z-values (elevation) as well. But this is mostly not the case.

trans-unit.source: CRS dropdown: Select the EPSG/CRS you want to set for the new layer. By default, QGIS selects the project CRS. If you want to change the CRS click on ![](/fig/mIconProjectionEnabled.png).
trans-unit.target: CRS dropdown: Select the EPSG/CRS you want to set for the new layer. By default, QGIS selects the project CRS. If you want to change the CRS click on ![](/fig/mIconProjectionEnabled.png).

trans-unit.source: Under `New Field` you can add columns to the new layer. Here you can set up what other type of data you want to collect in this dataset.
trans-unit.target: Under `New Field` you can add columns to the new layer. Here you can set up what other type of data you want to collect in this dataset.

trans-unit.source: `Type`: Select the data type the column will have e.g. `Text`, `Whole number`, `Decimal Number`, `Date`.
trans-unit.target: `Type`: Select the data type the column will have e.g. `Text`, `Whole number`, `Decimal Number`, `Date`.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png) to add the new column to the `Fields List`.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionNewAttribute.png) to add the new column to the `Fields List`.

trans-unit.source: Click `OK` to create the new data.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK` to create the new data.

trans-unit.source: The Layer Creation window in QGIS 3.36. :::
trans-unit.target: The Layer Creation window in QGIS 3.36. :::

trans-unit.source: Once you have set up the new layer, you can start adding geometrical features. The process for the three geometric types is basically the same:
trans-unit.target: Once you have set up the new layer, you can start adding geometrical features. The process for the three geometric types is basically the same:

trans-unit.source: Creating new data entries
trans-unit.target: Creating new data entries

trans-unit.source: Select the layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing`. Make sure the layer is in the editing mode. If not, click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon in the digitisation toolbar.
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing`. Make sure the layer is in the editing mode. If not, click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon in the digitisation toolbar.

trans-unit.source: Creating point data
trans-unit.target: Creating point data

trans-unit.source: Select the point layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the point layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing`. Make sure the layer is in the editing mode. If not, click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon in the digitisation toolbar.
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing`. Make sure the layer is in the editing mode. If not, click on the ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) icon in the digitisation toolbar.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).

trans-unit.source: Left-click on the feature you want to digitise.
trans-unit.target: Left-click on the feature you want to digitise.

trans-unit.source: Once you click, a window will appear `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute`. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.
trans-unit.target: Once you click, a window will appear `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute`. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with digitisation ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with digitisation ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: How to create point data
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: How to create point data
:::

trans-unit.source: Point creation. :::
trans-unit.target: Point creation. :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Getting coordinates from google maps :class: tip
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Getting coordinates from google maps :class: tip

trans-unit.source: Sometimes, the easiest way to get the coordinates for a location, such as the office of a national Red Cross or Red Crescent branch or simply of a house, is to use google maps. In Google Maps, you can right click on any location to get the coordinates (in degrees).
trans-unit.target: Sometimes, the easiest way to get the coordinates for a location, such as the office of a national Red Cross or Red Crescent branch or simply of a house, is to use google maps. In Google Maps, you can right click on any location to get the coordinates (in degrees).

trans-unit.source: In Google Maps, locate the point you wish to add to your QGIS project.
trans-unit.target: In Google Maps, locate the point you wish to add to your QGIS project.

trans-unit.source: Right click on the point and select the coordinates. They will be copied to your clipboard automatically.
trans-unit.target: Right click on the point and select the coordinates. They will be copied to your clipboard automatically.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to your QGIS project and paste the coordinates into the search bar in the bottom left corner of the QGIS window.
trans-unit.target: Navigate to your QGIS project and paste the coordinates into the search bar in the bottom left corner of the QGIS window.

trans-unit.source: Select `Go to [Your coordinates] (EPSG 4326: WGS 84)`.
trans-unit.target: Select `Go to [Your coordinates] (EPSG 4326: WGS 84)`.

trans-unit.source: The coordinate point will flash red on the map canvas. ::::
trans-unit.target: The coordinate point will flash red on the map canvas. ::::

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn!
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Now it's your turn!

trans-unit.source: Try digitising the RCRC branches in your country by following the steps below.
trans-unit.target: Try digitising the RCRC branches in your country by following the steps below.

trans-unit.source: Create a new point dataset.
trans-unit.target: Create a new point dataset.

trans-unit.source: Add a [basemap](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_basemap.md) (OSM or Bing Aerial, for example).
trans-unit.target: Add a [basemap](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_basemap.md) (OSM or Bing Aerial, for example).

trans-unit.source: Search the RCRC branches in your country on google maps.
trans-unit.target: Search the RCRC branches in your country on google maps.

trans-unit.source: Once you have located the branches, right-click on a branch in google maps and click on the coordinates. The coordinates will be copied to your clipboard.
trans-unit.target: Once you have located the branches, right-click on a branch in google maps and click on the coordinates. The coordinates will be copied to your clipboard.

trans-unit.source: Paste the coordinates into the search bar on the bottom left of the QGIS window. Select navigate to coordinates. The location will be marked by a red dot.
trans-unit.target: Paste the coordinates into the search bar on the bottom left of the QGIS window. Select navigate to coordinates. The location will be marked by a red dot.

trans-unit.source: Enable the editing mode ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) in your new layer.
trans-unit.target: Enable the editing mode ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) in your new layer.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePoint.png).

trans-unit.source: Add the point feature at the location that was indicated.
trans-unit.target: Add the point feature at the location that was indicated.

trans-unit.source: Add the name of the RCRC branch.
trans-unit.target: Add the name of the RCRC branch.

trans-unit.source: Click `Ok`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Ok`.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to exit the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to exit the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Creating line and polygon layers
trans-unit.target: Creating line and polygon layers

trans-unit.source: The process of creating line or polygon layers is essentially the same as creating point data. The main difference is that instead of only adding one point, line and polygon geometries require several points (vertices). Each point you add is a vertex between two lines. In QGIS, you create lines and polygons by setting one point, and then another point connected to the previously added point. To finish adding the feature use the <kbd>Right mouse button</kbd>.
trans-unit.target: The process of creating line or polygon layers is essentially the same as creating point data. The main difference is that instead of only adding one point, line and polygon geometries require several points (vertices). Each point you add is a vertex between two lines. In QGIS, you create lines and polygons by setting one point, and then another point connected to the previously added point. To finish adding the feature use the <kbd>Right mouse button</kbd>.

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Creating line data
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Creating line data

trans-unit.source: Creating line data works in the same way as creating point data (see above). First, you must create a new line layer or use an existing one.
trans-unit.target: Creating line data works in the same way as creating point data (see above). First, you must create a new line layer or use an existing one.

trans-unit.source: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now, the layer is in the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now, the layer is in the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCaptureLine.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCaptureLine.png).

trans-unit.source: To digitise line features, click along the line. When you are done, right-click on the last point of the line to finish the feature.
trans-unit.target: To digitise line features, click along the line. When you are done, right-click on the last point of the line to finish the feature.

trans-unit.source: A window will appear `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute`. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.
trans-unit.target: A window will appear `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute`. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with digitisation, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) `Save Layer Edits` to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with digitisation, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) `Save Layer Edits` to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing` again to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing` again to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: :::{tab-item} Creating polygon data
trans-unit.target: :::{tab-item} Creating polygon data

trans-unit.source: The creation of polygon layers works in the same way as for point and line data.
trans-unit.target: The creation of polygon layers works in the same way as for point and line data.

trans-unit.source: Select the polygon layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the polygon layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now, the layer is in the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png). Now, the layer is in the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePolygon.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionCapturePolygon.png).

trans-unit.source: To digitise polygon features, left-click around the area you want do digitize. When you are done, right-click on the last point of the area to finish the feature.
trans-unit.target: To digitise polygon features, left-click around the area you want do digitize. When you are done, right-click on the last point of the area to finish the feature.

trans-unit.source: A window will appear `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute`. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.
trans-unit.target: A window will appear `[Your Layer Name]- Feature Attribute`. Here you can add the information about this feature to the different columns, based on the attribute table of the layer.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with digitisation ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with digitisation ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing` again to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing` again to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify sd-rounded-3 sd-border-2 __Real World Scenario 2/3__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify sd-rounded-3 sd-border-2 __Real World Scenario 2/3__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: The first thing you do is locate the village using GPS-coordinates that you enter in the bottom right corner of the QGIS window. Thankfully, the process of digitising is relatively easy since there is a recent satellite image provided by Microsoft Bing. You can load the satellite image using the __QuickMapServices__ and searching and adding the `Bing Aerial` Basemap. You can see the buildings and roads on the satellite image. The next step will be to create new layers: one for the roads and one for the buildings.
trans-unit.target: The first thing you do is locate the village using GPS-coordinates that you enter in the bottom right corner of the QGIS window. Thankfully, the process of digitising is relatively easy since there is a recent satellite image provided by Microsoft Bing. You can load the satellite image using the __QuickMapServices__ and searching and adding the `Bing Aerial` Basemap. You can see the buildings and roads on the satellite image. The next step will be to create new layers: one for the roads and one for the buildings.

trans-unit.source: Editing the Data
trans-unit.target: Editing the Data

trans-unit.source: In some cases, you might want to modify or correct vector data because of inaccuracies or changes. Editing vector data is done with the digitisation toolbar. In QGIS, you can edit both the geometries and the values in the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: In some cases, you might want to modify or correct vector data because of inaccuracies or changes. Editing vector data is done with the digitisation toolbar. In QGIS, you can edit both the geometries and the values in the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Editing Geometries
trans-unit.target: Editing Geometries

trans-unit.source: For all cases:
trans-unit.target: For all cases:

trans-unit.source: Select the layer you want to edit.
trans-unit.target: Select the layer you want to edit.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to activate the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to activate the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Do your changes.
trans-unit.target: Do your changes.

trans-unit.source: Save your edit by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png).
trans-unit.target: Save your edit by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png).

trans-unit.source: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: :::{Tip} You can use the ![](/fig/mActionUndo.png) & ![](/fig/mActionRedo.png) buttons to reverse changes easily.
trans-unit.target: :::{Tip} You can use the ![](/fig/mActionUndo.png) & ![](/fig/mActionRedo.png) buttons to reverse changes easily.

trans-unit.source: Note that this is only possible __before__ you save the changes. :::
trans-unit.target: Note that this is only possible __before__ you save the changes. :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Deleting features
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Deleting features

trans-unit.source: Select the layer you want to modify.
trans-unit.target: Select the layer you want to modify.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing`.
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) `Toggle Editing`.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png) and select the feature you want to delete (see the [wiki](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html#manual-selection)).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionSelectRectangle.png) and select the feature you want to delete (see the [wiki](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html#manual-selection)).

trans-unit.source: Once you have selected the features, click on ![](/fig/mActionDeleteSelectedFeatures.png) to delete the feature.
trans-unit.target: Once you have selected the features, click on ![](/fig/mActionDeleteSelectedFeatures.png) to delete the feature.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with editing, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with editing, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click again on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Moving features
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Moving features

trans-unit.source: There are multiple methods to move features. Here we show the method that works the same for point, line, and polygon features. To do this, you need the Advanced Digitisation Toolbox.
trans-unit.target: There are multiple methods to move features. Here we show the method that works the same for point, line, and polygon features. To do this, you need the Advanced Digitisation Toolbox.

trans-unit.source: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionMoveFeaturePoint.png) and click on the feature you want to move. Then, click on the location where you want to move the feature.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionMoveFeaturePoint.png) and click on the feature you want to move. Then, click on the location where you want to move the feature.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with editing, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with editing, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Modifying geometries
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Modifying geometries

trans-unit.source: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Navigate to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).
trans-unit.target: Navigate to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionVertexToolActiveLayer.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionVertexToolActiveLayer.png).

trans-unit.source: Now you can now move every vertex (corner) of a feature. Click on the vertex/corner you want to move and then click on the location where you want to move the vertex to.
trans-unit.target: Now you can now move every vertex (corner) of a feature. Click on the vertex/corner you want to move and then click on the location where you want to move the vertex to.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with editing click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with editing click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: ::::{tab-item} Adding rings to polygon features
trans-unit.target: ::::{tab-item} Adding rings to polygon features

trans-unit.source: A ring in QGIS is a part inside a polygon that is not part of the polygon. Image a polygon representing a lake. The ring is an island in the lake. For a better understanding, watch the video below.
trans-unit.target: A ring in QGIS is a part inside a polygon that is not part of the polygon. Image a polygon representing a lake. The ring is an island in the lake. For a better understanding, watch the video below.

trans-unit.source: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.
trans-unit.target: Select the line layer you want to add data to in the Layer panel.

trans-unit.source: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).
trans-unit.target: Go to the digitisation toolbar and click on![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).

trans-unit.source: Click on ![add ring icon](/fig/mActionAddRing.png).
trans-unit.target: Click on ![add ring icon](/fig/mActionAddRing.png).

trans-unit.source: Create a ring by clicking the area you want to exclude. Right-click to close the ring.
trans-unit.target: Create a ring by clicking the area you want to exclude. Right-click to close the ring.

trans-unit.source: Once you are done with editing, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Once you are done with editing, click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to end the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to end the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Editing the attribute values
trans-unit.target: Editing the attribute values

trans-unit.source: Sometimes, you will have to edit the values in the columns of the attribute table. For example, the name of an administrative district is written incorrectly or differently, or a value has not been entered correctly. To do this, you will have to:
trans-unit.target: Sometimes, you will have to edit the values in the columns of the attribute table. For example, the name of an administrative district is written incorrectly or differently, or a value has not been entered correctly. To do this, you will have to:

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} You can open the attribute table of the selected layer by pressing <kbd>F6</kbd>. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} You can open the attribute table of the selected layer by pressing <kbd>F6</kbd>. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: Open the [attribute table](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_attribute_table.md).
trans-unit.target: Open the [attribute table](/content/Module_2/en_qgis_attribute_table.md).

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to enter into the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) to enter into the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: Choose the field that you want to edit.
trans-unit.target: Choose the field that you want to edit.

trans-unit.source: Enter the corrected value.
trans-unit.target: Enter the corrected value.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits.

trans-unit.source: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to exit the editing mode.
trans-unit.target: Click on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) again to exit the editing mode.

trans-unit.source: This process is called __"Data cleaning"__ and is important when performing data analysis or manipulating data in any way. While collecting or digitising data, it is easy to make small mistakes, such as a wrong value, wrong value type, or a spelling mistake. When performing analyses, it is therefore important to investigate the attribute table for inconsistencies or errors. If these errors are not cleaned, the results will be incorrect and you might draw the wrong conclusions!
trans-unit.target: This process is called __"Data cleaning"__ and is important when performing data analysis or manipulating data in any way. While collecting or digitising data, it is easy to make small mistakes, such as a wrong value, wrong value type, or a spelling mistake. When performing analyses, it is therefore important to investigate the attribute table for inconsistencies or errors. If these errors are not cleaned, the results will be incorrect and you might draw the wrong conclusions!

trans-unit.source: ::::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify sd-text-black sd-rounded-3 sd-border-2 __Real world Scenario 3/3__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{card} :class-card: sd-text-justify sd-text-black sd-rounded-3 sd-border-2 __Real world Scenario 3/3__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: With the new layers, you are ready to trace the buildings and roads in the new layers. You already have some knowledge about the condition of the roads (e.g., the road surface, quality, and if it is flooded) and the condition of the houses (e.g., if it is affected by a flood, if it has multiple stories, ...). This is useful information that can be stored in the additional attributes in the data table.
trans-unit.target: With the new layers, you are ready to trace the buildings and roads in the new layers. You already have some knowledge about the condition of the roads (e.g., the road surface, quality, and if it is flooded) and the condition of the houses (e.g., if it is affected by a flood, if it has multiple stories, ...). This is useful information that can be stored in the additional attributes in the data table.

trans-unit.source: Building Damage Assessment in Paikgachha Upazila, Khulna District, Khulna Division, Bangladesh as of 4 June 2024 (Source: [Int'l Charter, UNOSAT](https://reliefweb.int/map/bangladesh/building-damage-assessment-paikgachha-upazila-khulna-district-khulna-division-bangladesh-4-june-2024-imagery-analysis-4-june-2024-published-7-june-2024-v1)) :::
trans-unit.target: Building Damage Assessment in Paikgachha Upazila, Khulna District, Khulna Division, Bangladesh as of 4 June 2024 (Source: [Int'l Charter, UNOSAT](https://reliefweb.int/map/bangladesh/building-damage-assessment-paikgachha-upazila-khulna-district-khulna-division-bangladesh-4-june-2024-imagery-analysis-4-june-2024-published-7-june-2024-v1)) :::

trans-unit.source: Spatial Digitisation Errors in QGIS
trans-unit.target: Spatial Digitisation Errors in QGIS

trans-unit.source: The accuracy of geodata is crucial for spatial analysis. Positional errors are inevitable when data are manually digitised. The most common examples include undershooting and overshooting. When your coordinates do not connect as they should, and overshooting, when the lines go past where they should. Often these errors are not visible unless you zoom in quite a bit on the coordinates. Setting a fuzzy tolerance (snapping tolerance) is used to reduce undershoots and overshoots. The snapping tolerance is the minimum tolerated distance between nodes, lines and/or vertices.
trans-unit.target: The accuracy of geodata is crucial for spatial analysis. Positional errors are inevitable when data are manually digitised. The most common examples include undershooting and overshooting. When your coordinates do not connect as they should, and overshooting, when the lines go past where they should. Often these errors are not visible unless you zoom in quite a bit on the coordinates. Setting a fuzzy tolerance (snapping tolerance) is used to reduce undershoots and overshoots. The snapping tolerance is the minimum tolerated distance between nodes, lines and/or vertices.

trans-unit.source: Digitising Errors (Source: SpatialPost). :::
trans-unit.target: Digitising Errors (Source: SpatialPost). :::

trans-unit.source: Self-Assessment Questions
trans-unit.target: Self-Assessment Questions

trans-unit.source: ::::{admonition} Recapitulate your knowledge :class: note
trans-unit.target: ::::{admonition} Recapitulate your knowledge :class: note

trans-unit.source: __What is digitisation in GIS? Why is converting features from maps or images into vector form useful?__
trans-unit.target: __What is digitisation in GIS? Why is converting features from maps or images into vector form useful?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer Digitisation (or “digitization”) in GIS is the process of tracing or capturing geographic features (points, lines, polygons) from maps, images (such as satellite or aerial imagery, scanned maps) __or reports__ and converting them into vector data. It is useful because it let's us perform spatial analyses and gain insights about the captured phenomenon in space. Furthermore, we can combine it with other spatial data and display it on informative maps. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer Digitisation (or “digitization”) in GIS is the process of tracing or capturing geographic features (points, lines, polygons) from maps, images (such as satellite or aerial imagery, scanned maps) __or reports__ and converting them into vector data. It is useful because it let's us perform spatial analyses and gain insights about the captured phenomenon in space. Furthermore, we can combine it with other spatial data and display it on informative maps. :::

trans-unit.source: __How do you create a new vector dataset?__
trans-unit.target: __How do you create a new vector dataset?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer

trans-unit.source: In the top bar, navigate to the `Layer` menu → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer` or `New Shapefile Layer`
trans-unit.target: In the top bar, navigate to the `Layer` menu → `Create Layer` → `New GeoPackage Layer` or `New Shapefile Layer`

trans-unit.source: Specify the layer properties:
trans-unit.target: Specify the layer properties:

trans-unit.source: Specify the file path and name
trans-unit.target: Specify the file path and name

trans-unit.source: **Encoding**: UTF-8 (allows for special characters)
trans-unit.target: **Encoding**: UTF-8 (allows for special characters)

trans-unit.source: **Geometry type**: point, line, polygon (one layer supports only one geometry type)
trans-unit.target: **Geometry type**: point, line, polygon (one layer supports only one geometry type)

trans-unit.source: **Coordinate Reference System (CRS)**
trans-unit.target: **Coordinate Reference System (CRS)**

trans-unit.source: Define the __attribute fields__ (columns) that you want in the layer's attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Define the __attribute fields__ (columns) that you want in the layer's attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Click `Ok` to create the create the layer. :::
trans-unit.target: Click `Ok` to create the create the layer. :::

trans-unit.source: __Imagine you’re digitising flood‑affected buildings from an aerial image. What attribute fields might you include, and what level of detail or accuracy would you try to maintain?__
trans-unit.target: __Imagine you’re digitising flood‑affected buildings from an aerial image. What attribute fields might you include, and what level of detail or accuracy would you try to maintain?__

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Answer Which attributes you wish to digitise always depends on the goal of your mapping effort (e.g. emergency response, damage assessment, or planning) and if you are on the ground or rely on satellite imagery.
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Answer Which attributes you wish to digitise always depends on the goal of your mapping effort (e.g. emergency response, damage assessment, or planning) and if you are on the ground or rely on satellite imagery.

trans-unit.source: __Potential attribute fields__:
trans-unit.target: __Potential attribute fields__:

trans-unit.source: __Building type/usage__: e.g. residential, commercial, industrial, public
trans-unit.target: __Building type/usage__: e.g. residential, commercial, industrial, public

trans-unit.source: __Number of stories/floors__
trans-unit.target: __Number of stories/floors__

trans-unit.source: __Roof material__
trans-unit.target: __Roof material__

trans-unit.source: __Wall material__
trans-unit.target: __Wall material__

trans-unit.source: __Damage status__: e.g. "no damage", "minor damage", "severe damage", "collapsed"
trans-unit.target: __Damage status__: e.g. "no damage", "minor damage", "severe damage", "collapsed"

trans-unit.source: __Flood depth or water exposure__
trans-unit.target: __Flood depth or water exposure__

trans-unit.source: __Date of assessment__
trans-unit.target: __Date of assessment__

trans-unit.source: __Confidence/quality__
trans-unit.target: __Confidence/quality__

trans-unit.source: __Address__
trans-unit.target: __Address__
